author: BBB complainant
review_text: Billing Issues. On Saturday 12-24-2026, I had submitted the document form to purchase a buyer account with REMOVED . Upon submission, an error message had occurred that there was a technical issue with payment and to try again later. After reviewing my input of financial information, I determined I had been correct with entering information and made the second attempt. Upon this second attempt, the same message occurs. I contacted support to help me obtain an upgraded public buyer's account, and during the wait time to connect with an agent, my banking account indicated that I had actually been charged twice already the initial $200 fees to yield for my account purchase. I check my profile and after charges were made, my account still states it's in guest status. After speaking with support, I was told that my transactions were not accepted by IAA due to them being voided, although I did not void them upon my behalf and the funds were instantly debited from my account to REMOVED . I was told that because they were voided, the bank will reissue my funds to credit my account within 2-3 days at max and IAA never received them. It has now been 5 days, I've spoken to support via live chat and by text message without any difference of help, and I still do not have my $400 that was taken from me. It's quite evident that IAA did in fact receive my funds and are refusing to refund my money. If my transactions were truly voided as they say, then generally in my experience a voided transaction is processed that same day. It's been almost a week now and I've grown impatient with the company. I no longer desire to do business with this establishment and after reading many other reviews at any given branch, I wouldn't recommend them to anyone as their conduct of operations appear to be faulty and, in my case, fraudulent. I request I receive compensation promptly for my losses immediately, and if my request is not met, legal action will be pursued. This type of conduct is unacceptable and is considered theft.

After getting payment, she forced us to get vibrant color waist otherwise she wouldnt let us get the vehicle. She refused us to let use the one that was hanging around that no one was using. We explained her that the red shirt I was wearing also considered as vibrant color but she refused to accept any of the fact and kept saying that if we will argue, she would ask us to leave. When I asked to speak with a manager, thats where she said shes the manager there. As I walked out, some other customer gave me their waist to make thing easy so she finally moved on from that argument but she made it clear that no one would help us with camper. Anyway, we got the camper and thats where we realized there are more issues than advertised. Camper wires were cut-off, no battery and trailer REMOVED was bent (at least that was shown in pics) so it wasnt easy to move the camper. We asked for help and were told that we should have been prepared as these guys dont help with it. Somehow we brought camper home and later realized that it wasnt even winterized as shown in the pictures. Water tank, heater still had fresh water in it that bushed the pipes and leaked all around, that I made video and took pictures including issues with cut-off wiring. Called IAAI on Dec 24th and spoke with agent name REMOVED on this. He escalated to their upper management and theyll get back to me within REMOVED hours under Case No. REMOVED . author: BBB complainant
review_text: Billing Issues. Subject: Formal Complaint Against IAAI Account Closure Without Explanation I am submitting this formal complaint against IAAI (Insurance Auto Auctions) regarding the sudden and unjustified closure of my account without any clear explanation or prior notice.I was not contacted in advance, nor was I given any opportunity to understand the reason for the closure or to defend myself. When I attempted to reach customer support, I did not receive a clear explanation, and no fair or impartial investigation appears to have been conducted. It seems that this decision was made based solely on an internal report without reviewing my side of the matter.This action has caused me significant financial harm, as I rely on my IAAI account as a source of income, in addition to emotional and psychological stress due to the lack of transparency and fairness in handling this situation.Through this complaint, I respectfully request:A written and official explanation for the account closure Confirmation of whether any proper investigation was conducted A review of the decision and reinstatement of my account if no wrongdoing is found I am willing to fully cooperate with any fair investigation and have attached any relevant communications or supporting documents.Thank you for your time and assistance.

author: BBB complainant
review_text: Sales and Advertising Issues. Business Name: IAA (Insurance Auto Auctions) Gardena, CA BranchComplainant: REMOVED (Buyer # REMOVED )Case Reference: IAA Case # REMOVED I purchased a 2011 Nissan Rogue from IAAs Gardena auction on 8/15/2025. The vehicle was misrepresented in the listing, and IAA has refused to address the issue.The Rogue was advertised as REMOVED Normal REMOVED with a 49/50 Vehicle Score (suggesting only minor cosmetic issues). Upon delivery through IAAs own transport, the vehicle had a failed transmission and could not move under its own power. This is completely inconsistent with the advertised condition.Additionally, the Seller Info section was blank in the listing. Only after delivery did I discover the seller was REMOVED , a broker that buys unwanted cars from the public, often vehicles with hidden mechanical problems. This was a material omission that prevented me from making an informed decision.I opened a case with IAA ( REMOVED ), but the branch manager dismissed it with boilerplate as-is language and never addressed the misrepresentation or seller nondisclosure. This has caused me financial loss (nearly $400 transport plus a non-drivable vehicle) and undermined trust in IAAs advertised scoring and listing system.Resolution Requested: I am seeking reversal of the sale or a financial adjustment/credit. At minimum, I request acknowledgment from IAA that their listing was misleading and failed to disclose seller identity.

author: BBB complainant
review_text: Product Issues. Member REMOVED . I wired the amount of $6279.50, kept my money hostage for 4 more days after receiving and clearing. Stay away from these scammers! We are a decade long member with IAA, and I manage many accounts with them. We have made wire transfers for our payments for many years and it normally took up to 2 hours for the wires to be posted. We use a same day Bofa to Bofa, the most expensive expedited wire method available. Due to low revenue, they have started to hold member's payments hostage for up to 72 hours so they make money on 2.5% late fees and whopping $50 dollar a day storage fees. Their team is especially encouraged not to release the funds on Thursdays and Fridays to keep your vehicle over the weekend - that is a guaranteed $150 for them on storage fees. We plan sending transporters the next day when we send wires, and dozens of frustrating cancellation cases because of their scam (and yes in most cases we have to cover transporter's dry run). The company have lost their culture and team members are told to hang up the phone on members when conversation isn't going their way. Zero attempts by team members to try to resolve your issue, they will talk all over you, raise their voice and hang up. No manager available at all times. Not to mention, if your bank sends over the wires with a misspell or with no name description, they will never post your wire, and we have waited up to 3 months to get our money back. Good luck paying late fees, storage fees and 10% relist fees in the meantime.

author: BBB complainant
review_text: Sales and Advertising Issues. purchased a vehicle through Insurance Auto Auctions (IAA). The vehicle was sold with the representation that it came with a valid, transferable title. After the purchase, we resold the vehicle to a third-party buyer located in REMOVED , REMOVED On April 11, 2025, the buyer went to the Arizona DMV to register the vehicle and presented the title that was provided by IAA. However, the REMOVED informed them that the title had been invalidated due to a duplicate title request submitted on April 8, 2025after IAA had already sold the vehicle.Neither we nor our buyer requested this duplicate title. In fact, the buyer still possesses the original title issued by IAA. According to REMOVED policy, a duplicate title cannot be issued unless the original is surrendered. Therefore, the only plausible explanation is that IAA or the previous titled owner (LeasePlan USA) submitted the request, which has now caused a serious legal and financial problem.Our buyer is unable to register the vehicle and is demanding a refund. We have reached out to IAA multiple times and have yet to receive a resolution or a valid replacement title. Under California law, a seller is legally obligated to provide a clear and transferable title at the time of sale. Failing to do so is a breach of that obligation.Resolution Requested:We are requesting that IAA immediately take responsibility for this error by:Investigating and confirming who submitted the duplicate title request on April 8, 2025.Providing a valid, transferable title for the vehicle without further delay.If a valid title cannot be delivered, issuing a full refund to the buyer.This situation has caused significant hardship, and we urge IAA to act swiftly to correct it.

author: BBB complainant
review_text: Sales and Advertising Issues. 3-18-25 Better Business Bureau I am writing to file a formal complaint against Insurance REMOVED an auto auction service that I believe is engaging in unethical and deceptive business practices. As a consumer, I am extremely dissatisfied with their operations, My complaint concerns the auction of vehicles by this company, which I have reason to believe are being sold in poor condition, often with undisclosed damage, and in some cases, they are simply not functional. These vehicles are being auctioned off to buyers who, in good faith, expect to receive a vehicle that has been accurately described. However, it is becoming increasingly evident that the vehicles being sold at this auction are either faulty or severely damaged without informing the consumer.Moreover, the company seems to have a business model that prioritizes collecting auction fees from unsuspecting buyers, rather than ensuring the vehicles meet a reasonable standard of quality or transparency. To make matters worse, when these damaged vehicles are subsequently resold back through the auction by the new owner, IAA reportedly wants to submit claims for the very damages that should have been disclosed at the time of the original sale.This practice not only misleads buyers, but it also perpetuates an environment of dishonesty that puts consumers at risk. It appears that the auction companys primary interest lies in collecting auction fees without regard for the welfare or interests of their clients. This behavior is not only unethical but also, in my opinion, illegal under consumer protection laws.I respectfully request that the Better Business Bureau investigate these practices and hold INSURANCE AUTO AUCTION accountable for its actions. It is crucial that this company be made aware that deceptive and faulty sales practices are unacceptable in the marketplace, and that they must be held to higher standards of accountability and transparency.Sincerely, REMOVED
tier1: Lot Condition, Listing Status and related

author: BBB complainant
review_text: Service or Repair Issues. Sometime during the last week of August 2024 Insurance Auto Auctions (IAA) database was hacked and their customers personal information was obtained. Using this information, these hackers were able to illigally purchase vehicles on IAA's platform using their customers credentials and banking information. Multiple accounts were impacted and most of the vehicle purchases occured on the east coast. Through conversations with IAA I was informed that there were so many accounts that were impacted that they were using a spreadsheet to track all of the fraudulant purchases. This information was not supposed to be divulged to me. When I spoke with IAA about this I was informed that I am responsible for the security of my account, and as a result, I was responsible for the purchases. Since those initial phone calls, IAA REMOVED have been told they could no longer speak with me and to let me know my account was under review. My account has now been under review for 6 months and still no one will speak with me. Also since this has occured, IAA has implemented 2 step verification on all accounts. I assume this is a reaction to what happened to the breached accounts. The fact the IAA is taking no responsiblilty for what happened, or speaking with their customers on how to resolve this issue, is what bothers me the most. All that I am looking for is for them to admit that I do not owe them $125,000 and that they actually still owe me the $20,000 I was unable to recover.

She said they had done a thorough search & the car was empty and there was nothing they would do. I complained to State Farm who reached out to IAA again. IAA checked my car again and found more items (but not all) and sent them. 3/6 I spoke to IAA Branch Manager REMOVED . Told him I am still missing approx $150 worth of items & believe they were stolen. He said they don't have them. Then admitted they did have my car key for a different car and agreed to send it. I am still missing approximately $150 worth of items. IAA never had permission to take my car but they chose to take it knowing that my items were inside so they are responsible for my items. Also, it has taken me three different complaints to get some of the items returned.

author: BBB complainant
review_text: Service or Repair Issues. IAAI's reciept is deceptive. The reciept lists "sales tax" but does not identify which state the sales tax was paid to. In this instance they charged sales tax for the state where the sale originated ( REMOVED ) and not the state where the vehicle was delivered ( REMOVED ) which is not consistent with conventional standards. As with all internet sales ( REMOVED , REMOVED , etsy etc) sales tax is due at the delivery location and not at the origination location. I am willing to pay Utah sales tax. As IAAI has offices in REMOVED this should have been a simple thing to do, but when I called IAAI and talked to Tashoy on 3/12/25 to explain that sales tax is paid at the delivery location and not at the origination location she hung up on me.

author: BBB complainant
review_text: Service or Repair Issues. I had the worst experience with IAA REMOVED in REMOVED , REMOVED , after I crashed my Jeep and it ended up in their hands. To say Im furious would be an understatementthis place turned a bad situation into a complete disaster. My Jeep was totaled in an accident, and I figured IAA, being a big-name auto auction company, would handle it professionally. Boy, was I wrong. First off, when I went to retrieve my personal belongings from the wreckage, half my stuff was missing. I had tools, some electronics, and even sentimental items in theregone. I asked the staff about it, and they just shrugged, acting like it wasnt their problem. Then, to top it off, I noticed my license plate was missing too. I mean, who steals a license plate from a totaled car? Its not like theyre reselling it at auction! Im convinced someone at IAA swiped it, either for personal use or to cover up something shady. Its left me paranoid that my plates out there being used for who-knows-what. The whole process was a mess from start to finish. The staff couldnt care lessrude, unhelpful, and totally dismissive when I tried to get answers. Ive heard stories online about IAA lots having issues with lost property, and now Im a believer. Its like they see a crashed car as a free-for-all to pick through before the owner gets there. And dont get me started on communicationI called multiple times to figure out what happened, left messages, and got zero response. Its as if they ghost you once theyve got your vehicle. The whole vibe screams neglect, and its clear they dont care about the people who actually own these vehiclesjust the profit they can squeeze out of them. If youve got a wrecked car heading to IAA REMOVED in REMOVED , good luck. Im still dealing with the fallout of replacing my stolen plate and lost property, and I wouldnt wish this headache on anyone. Avoid this place if you canits a black hole of incompetence and shady behavior.

In September of 2024 I attempted to transfer the title to my daughter and the REMOVED told me the vehicle needed a salvage title and the title I had was not valid. I had to mail a form and the title to the State with a check, to request a salvage title. I did that and 6 weeks later received a rejection letter from the State of Texas stating "Texas does not issue titles for hail damage only vehicles. Please contact your insurance company to send in a VTR-436-E if the information is incorrect to correct change"I have had multiple conversations with USAA in which they have conferenced in IAA and this has not been resolved. Today REMOVED called me and said that IAA is saying that the State of Texas has changed some rules regarding salvage titles and the new rules shouldn't apply to my title because it was salvaged prior to the change. I informed her that the State of Texas hasn't required a salvage title for hail damaged vehicles since 2017, well before this claim, so someone at IAA is lying. She said it has been escalated to the Process Manager, REMOVED , at IAA; I asked her to get a message to REMOVED that I would like her to call me. author: BBB complainant
review_text: Sales and Advertising Issues. My 2014 Jeep Grand Cherokee Summit. In May of this year a tree fell from a neighbors property and broke the passenger side window of my Jeep. I happened to be out of town at the time during frequent storms and there was some water damage on some of the dash and center console.I made a claim with my insurance company. They seemed to had a difficult time finding someone who would quote the electrical issues caused by this. After sometime, they asked if they "could take my vehicle to their shop in REMOVED , Ga to get a damage appraisal". I found out later that my vehicle wasn't taken to REMOVED , it was taken to IAA in REMOVED , REMOVED . My insurance company paid IAA to pick up my vehicle, do the appraisal, and bring it back. Apparently they were unable to do anything other than note the broken window on the repair estimate. So the vehicle was towed to My Mechanic to troubleshoot and undergo repairs. When my vehicle got to the shop, my mechanic called me and asked if it had any body damage or had the front bumper been ripped off. "ABSOLUTELY NOT!" I replied and came down to the shop to see what had happened. Before it left I had the battery disconnected to possibly save the modules from further damage. Therefore it wouldnt start. The Tow driver told me that each time they had to move my vehicle they use giant fork lift and that's what ripped the bumper. I immediately called IAA and spoke with REMOVED . He denied everything, then placed blame on the tow company. Come to find out my insurance paid them directly for the tow and the appraisal. So either way it was on them. REMOVED then told me to get an estimate for damages. My mechanic replaced and fixed all the original damage from the tree and documented the undercarriage damage. The estimate from the body shop was ONLY for the forklift damage. The estimate totaled $11,701.02 and when I submitted that to REMOVED he said "we're not paying for that". My Jeep came to them with ONLY a broken window/interior

author: BBB complainant
review_text: Service or Repair Issues. I am writing to formally lodge a complaint against IAA regarding a recent vehicle purchase that has been fraught with issues. My grievance concerns significant discrepancies between the advertised condition of the vehicle and its actual state upon arrival to pick up the vehicle, as well as the company's refusal to address these issues satisfactorily.On Sept. 10 2024, I purchased a vehicle from IAA which was advertised with a video demonstrating that it was in running condition, with the primary damage described as normal wear and tear. Based on this representation, I proceeded with the purchase.However, when the transporter arrived to collect the vehicle, it was clear that the vehicle was not as advertised. The vehicle failed to start, and upon closer inspection, it was evident that the engine harness had been severely damagedevidence suggesting possible mishandling or damage during IAA's possession.Despite multiple attempts to resolve the issue with IAA, they have maintained that their responsibility extends only to the condition of the vehicle at the time of arrival, not accounting for the subsequent damage incurred while the vehicle was under their control. They have refused to issue a refund or credit, citing their policy that absolves them of responsibility for any changes in condition post-arrival.This situation is both frustrating and unfair. The vehicle was misrepresented, and IAAs failure to acknowledge and address the damages incurred under their custody is unacceptable. I am seeking your assistance in resolving this matter. I believe that IAA should be held accountable for the discrepancies between their advertisement and the actual condition of the vehicle, and I am requesting a full refund or appropriate compensation for the damages sustained.Thank you for your attention to this matter. I look forward to your support in achieving a fair resolution.

The word Run means the engine of a vehicle turns on and stays running, the word DRIVE means the vehicle moves forward and reverses. IaaI aka REMOVED are running a complete Scam auction in which they not only lie about the condition of the vehicles their selling but they also bid on the vehicles themselves using overseas accounts, in order to increase the bidding amount. If you go on Iaai website you will also see the words normal wear and tear next to their vehicles but if you check the sound of the engine video ALL of their engines clearly have a knock or tapping sound which indicates engine damage. IAAi needs to remove RUN AND DRIVE, NORMAL WEAR AND TEAR from their announcements immediately.

author: BBB complainant
review_text: Service or Repair Issues. On 1-6-24 we agreed to sell our 2013 REMOVED Equinox for $1040 guaranteed (documented). We set up a pickup date of 1-12-24 between the hours of 12pm-5pm. On 1-11-24 the pickup appointment was confirmed. By 4:30pm on 1-12-24 we had to contact the pickup company of IAA to see if they were still coming, and they couldnt find our vehicle in the records but assured us they would still send someone out with a check in hand. The driver arrived to pickup our Equinox and took our title as well as our vehicle, but handed us a check that didnt have our full names on it, rendering that check useless. We were unable to deposit the check due to the clerical error, and now we also dont have our vehicle or title.When the error was brought to the attention of IAA, we were told we could bring the check with errors to their office on Monday to rectify the errors and get a new check. On Monday 1-15-24 we were told they would void the old check and send us a new one via REMOVED overnight. On 1-16-24 we had to call IAA once again and were told they were voiding the original check and we would receive a call later that day letting us know the new check was available for pickup. No one called.We called back and were now told that they havent voided the old check and will only issue a new check 24hours after the old check is voided, but we cant come pick it up today. We asked if we can get our check tomorrow, 1-17-24, but were told they dont know. So now we have no car, no title and no check from our business transaction with IAA. The clerical error is their fault, but we have nothing to show for it except a check with incomplete information that looks suspiciously fraudulent since the invoice and check dont have complete information. At this point Im pretty sure we got taken advantage of and scammed.I will never recommend this business to anyone.

The receptionists at this location were very rude and unprofessional during this time. I then went to recover my car on August 29, I was there at my set appointment time, I was forced to wait almost two hours to get the car, even though I got there early. I have a 6 month old baby, and it was over 100 degrees that day. I went outside to finally get my car, and EVERYTHING in the car had been removed. The only thing the two men didn't remove was my hidden dash camera in my review mirror. So I got home and saw the footage of them ripping my custom leather seat covers, leaving giant holes. My license plate covers had been torn off, even though they could easily be removed without tearing them. A priceless necklace my deceased grandfather gave me was cut off the review mirror, my computer equipment was dropped and busted the bottom of my tower. My eight year old daughter's REMOVED was stolen, as was money from my center console. Who knows how many others these employees do this to, and those people just don't have proof like I do. I called to see what could be done about my broken and stolen items, and the two women I spoke to were extremely unprofessional and hateful to me. That's when I noticed the water stain on the fabric. I then ran the REMOVED through through other sites recommended by NMVTIS. Three of the sites reported the car had been declared a salvage vehicle by IAA REMOVED Brothers) out of REMOVED . REMOVED denied having knowledge of that during numerous phone conversations. I contacted IAA, but they wouldn't give me any information, saying I wasn't their buyer. Upon my request to dealer, he got a letter from them on their letterhead stating the car had a clean title. A body shop/garage here examined the vehicle and took 9 photos of evidence of flood damage, which I forwarded to dealer. He eventually agreed to buy the car back, plus pay extra for travel expenses stating, "I'll just be able to sell it for more to someone else." If he was a reputable dealer and truly hadn't known about the flood damage, his reaction should've been that he was going to take the matter up with REMOVED about the evidence I had found on the car and online. Definite shady business practices all around.

author: Jennifer F
review_text: I was in a car accident on July 7, 2023. I took pictures of the vehicle before it was taken to the wreckage company. A few days after the accident, my truck was transferred from the wreckage company to IAA due to REMOVED insurance reporting my vehicle as total loss. When I received the report for the settlement offer I noticed additional damages or so called "conditions" that devalued the amount that they were offering me. So, I called REMOVED and requested the IAA pictures that they had for the inspection. Turns out that my stereo and backup camera were robbed. The whole front dashboard panel was damaged b/c they had to open it to unhook the wires of the stereo. The whole center console was removed and replaced with a different one. The front seats I originally had on my truck were leather and on the pictures of IAA it shows cloth seats. The passenger door appears to be swapped with another vehicle's door because in the bottom I can see that it's damaged and the handle of the door is black and the side mirror of the door is white when my truck originally had silver handles and the side mirror was black. The tailgate was also swapped with another vehicle's damaged tailgate because the tailgate handle was missing and my tailgate wasn't heavily chipped and it did have a tailgate handle and there was a backup camera on top of the license plate. I showed the pictures that I took on the day of the accident to the insurance company to compare it to the ones they have and they stated that they can't compensate me for those damages due to the fact that they're not responsible for it. Which makes no sense to me because they send my vehicle to this location. I'm requesting to have the inbound video reviewed to check which of your employer's stole from my vehicle and exchanged parts. I think it's unbelievable how someone that it was ok to remove and REMOVED of my truck because it was totaled when the vehicle had still NOT BEEN EVALUATED yet by the insurance company.

review_text: Service or Repair Issues. This business steals peoples personal possessions, and its employees lie with every breath of their being! On Monday, July 17, 2023 (after them being conveniently closed on weekends), I was finally allowed access to my vehicle to retrieve my personal belongings, only to find that it had been picked clean of any, and everything of valueincluding my 9mm handgun that was in the center console! When I asked an employee what happened to my belongings, I was told that that was how it arrived to their lotcompletely empty( no insurance information, registration, tag receipts, all of which were most assuredly in the glove compartment). After calling a Sheriffs Deputy to the scene; miraculously, an employee and a supposed supervisor brought me a garbage bag containing all of the vehicles paperwork I had previously mentioned, but still no gun! I have since called this business DAILY, trying to resolve this issue, and get reimbursed for the cost of the gun; yet they continue to operate with plausible deniability, despite the numerous BBB complaints that have been filed against them. How is this possible?

I then call IAA and ask them what's going on a younger girl picked up and said it's part of their procedure to detail cars and put personal items in a clear bag in the trunk, i explained the situationand she said to file a claim. I then asked to get a hold of the branch manager REMOVED . It takes her a week or more to even answer my calls or email me back. Then REMOVED gets incontact with me and says "it's under investigation". Then I get an email saying that from the pictures they can just give me $250 even though my stuff cost $450 added up. I asked to see pictures that they have off my car and since then no response. My items were stolen and no one is being held accountable, I've received no money. Please I need your help.

author: BBB complainant
review_text: Customer Service Issues. My vehicle was towed by REMOVED and delivered to IAA South on 5/20/2023. The vehicle, a 2011 Infiniti 37 Journey was processed officially into their system on 5/22/2023. On 5/30/2023, I went to IAA to pickup my vehicle and to my surprise there was considerable damage done to my vehicle. The front bumper, the grill and the fog lights were all gone and nowhere to be found. Upon further inspection of the vehicle, the front tires looked bent at the axel, the front quarter panel on the passenger side of the vehicle was pulled away from the frame, there was a white streak on the rear driver's side door with a dent (I have a photo of a white vehicle on the lot parked next to my vehicle, which appears my vehicle was thrown into). When I opened the inside of the vehicle, there were three perfectly sliced cuts into the driver side seat and all of my belongings were not in the vehicle.The condition my vehicle left my possession was nowhere near the condition the vehicle was returned to me after being in the custody, care and control of IAA. Understandably, I was livid and went inside to find out what happened to my vehicle, where were my belongings, why was I not called and informed about the damage, as well as why did my car look like it was in the process of being scraped for parts. I was told to fill out a claim form, which did not give me enough room to detail anything that was visibly wrong with my vehicle. My vehicle looks like it was in a major accident.
